---
title: "exampleABMRS"
author: "Junhui He"
output: github_document
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

# exampleEBARS

<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

The exampleABMRS consists of all R codes for numerical experiments in the article ***Adaptive Bayesian Multivariate Spline Knot Inference with Prior Specifications on Model Complexity***. The source package can be found in [ABMRS](https://github.com/junhuihe2000/ABMRS). We implement the proposed method in knot inference and function fitting, and apply it to real-world datasets.

## Overview

1. `fitting` includes the evaluation of curve and surface fitting with jumping discontinuity.
2. `knot_inference` includes simulations of knot inference in linear spline models with one, two and four knots.
3. `real_data` includes the analysis of real datasets in univariate curve fitting: triceps skinfold thickness data.
